summary: chalk-utils postinstall exfiltrates browser credentials, crypto wallets, and developer secrets to hardcoded C2
details: The package's `postinstall` lifecycle script runs automatically on `npm install` and performs a full credential/wallet/secrets harvest against the installer's machine, then POSTs the collected data to a hardcoded attacker endpoint at `http://149.28.127.35:8888`. Specifically, postinstall.js reads browser SQLite databases (Chrome/Brave/Edge/Firefox `Login Data`, `Cookies`, `Web Data`), enumerates 71 crypto-wallet browser extension IDs (including MetaMask `nkbihfbeogaeaoehlefnkodbefgpgknn`) under `Local Extension Settings` to extract vault/mnemonic/privateKey/seed material, walks user home directories (`~/Documents`, `~/Desktop`, `~/Downloads`, `~/OneDrive`, `~/Dropbox`, `~/keys`, `~/wallet`, `~/crypto`) scanning files for BIP-39 seed phrases and EVM private keys, and collects `~/.npmrc`, `.env`, and other developer-secret files. The main entry `index.js` is a dummy stub whose in-source comment states `The real payload is in postinstall.js`, and the postinstall header explicitly reads `Full browser credential theft + wallet scanner. Silent. Zero trace. Exfiltrates everything to C2.` The package name `chalk-utils` is namespace confusion against the popular `chalk` package despite shipping none of its functionality.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
